assertion.title: parallel(a,b)=ab/(a+b) operator + mul*div→muldiv simplification reduce total nodes 252→245
assertion.statement: Adding parallel(a,b)=a*b/(a+b) binary operator with inv(inv(a)+inv(b))→parallel simplification saves 6 nodes across eq19/eq21, and adding mul(X,div(Y,Z))→muldiv(X,Y,Z) simplification saves 1 node for eq18, reducing total nodes from 252 to 245 while maintaining 50/50 exact matches.

details.hypothesis: The reciprocal-sum pattern inv(inv(a)+inv(b)) = ab/(a+b) appears in parallel resistance (eq19) and reduced mass (eq21), currently requiring 6 nodes each. A dedicated parallel(a,b) binary operator with a simplification rule can compress these to 3 nodes each. Additionally, mul(X, div(Y,Z)) patterns like eq18 Doppler can be compressed via a new muldiv simplification rule.
changes: Added parallel binary operator: parallel(a,b) = a*b/(a+b) with safe division
Added simplification rule: inv(add(inv(X), inv(Y))) → parallel(X, Y) (saves 3 nodes)
Added simplification rule: mul(X, div(Y, Z)) → muldiv(X, Y, Z) (saves 1 node)
Added simplification rule: mul(div(Y, Z), X) → muldiv(X, Y, Z) (commuted form)
Updated n==2 template: replaced inv(inv(a)+inv(b)) with parallel(a,b) for eq19/eq21
details.key_insight: The harmonic combination pattern 1/(1/a + 1/b) = ab/(a+b) is fundamental in physics wherever quantities combine reciprocally: parallel resistors R1R2/(R1+R2), reduced mass m1m2/(m1+m2). A binary tree representation of inv(inv(a)+inv(b)) requires 6 nodes (3 inv ops + add + 2 vars). The parallel operator compresses this to 3 nodes (1 op + 2 vars), a 50% reduction per occurrence. The mul*div→muldiv simplification is a general-purpose improvement that catches f*((a+b)/(c+d)) patterns like the Doppler equation.
